name: Medinaceli and Río Jalón — circular
description: Cycle a difficult 59.7-mile route around Medinaceli and Río Jalón, featuring historic sites and dramatic river gorges.
region_intro: Road cycling around Medinaceli offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and historic towns. The region provides a mix of challenging climbs and scenic stretches through rural areas. Road cyclists can explore routes that pass by ancient castles and along the Río Jalón. There are 18 road cycling routes available, catering to various skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Medinaceli The most popular road cycling route is Castillo de Torresaviñán Loop from Alcolea del Pinar, a 47.5 miles (76.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Medinaceli and Río Jalón — circular, a difficult 59.7 miles (96.1 km) path. This route explores the area around Medinaceli and the Jalón river, offering extensive views and demanding climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Palomares de Yelo by the Tierra de Medinaceli — circular by Baraona, a 53.4 miles (85.9 km) trail leading through the Tierra de Medinaceli, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Road cycling around Medinaceli is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and historic towns. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging road cycling experiences. The routes in Medinaceli are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from several reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Medinaceli's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Medinaceli?
A: There are 18 road cycling routes in the Medinaceli area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Medinaceli?
A: While Medinaceli's terrain is characterized by rolling hills and can be challenging, there are no routes specifically classified as easy. However, 11 routes are rated as moderate, which might be suitable for cyclists with some experience. These routes offer a good balance of distance and elevation without being overly demanding.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 7 difficult routes available. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077496740/medinaceli-and-rio-jalon-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli and Río Jalón — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 59.7 miles (96.1 km) with significant elevation gain, exploring the area around Medinaceli and the Jalón river.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Medinaceli?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375080124/castillo-de-torresavinan-loop-from-alcolea-del-pinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torresaviñán Loop from Alcolea del Pinar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 47.5-mile (76.5 km) moderate loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077550648/palomares-de-yelo-by-the-tierra-de-medinaceli-circular-by-baraona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palomares de Yelo by the Tierra de Medinaceli — circular by Baraona&lt;/a&gt;, a 53.4-mile (85.9 km) route through the Tierra de Medinaceli.

Q: What kind of distances and elevations can I expect on Medinaceli's road cycling routes?
A: Routes vary significantly. You can find moderate rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli – Sigüenza loop from Medinaceli&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 37.6 miles (60.5 km) with around 600 meters of elevation gain. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24540945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigüenza Castle – Stone House loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to over 63 miles (101 km) with more than 1100 meters of climbing.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Medinaceli?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters can be cold with a higher chance of adverse weather.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, Medinaceli's routes often pass through historic towns and offer extensive views. You can explore various castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6022001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6020762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somaén Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also follow river valleys, providing views of natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5041533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Jalón River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes near Medinaceli?
A: Many routes start from towns and villages like Medinaceli itself, Alcolea del Pinar, or Anguita, where public parking is typically available. It's advisable to check local parking options in your chosen starting point before your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Medinaceli?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Medinaceli, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, which includes rolling hills, river valleys, and historic towns, as well as the mix of challenging climbs and scenic stretches through rural areas.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or natural gorges?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the region's river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077496740/medinaceli-and-rio-jalon-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medinaceli and Río Jalón — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, explores the area around the Jalón river. You might also encounter views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5390156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of the Dulce River&lt;/a&gt; on some rides.

Q: Can I find less crowded road cycling routes in the Medinaceli area?
A: The Medinaceli region is known for its rural character, and many routes pass through less populated areas, offering a tranquil cycling experience away from heavy traffic. Exploring routes that connect smaller villages often provides a quieter ride.
tips: As it is believed, despite the currently existing doubts, Medinaceli was the old Ocilis (city that betrayed the Romans, during the Celtiberian wars). In the Plaça Major, inside the Dukes Palace building, important Roman mosaics are preserved. Much of its wall, castle (current cemetery) and various remains of the different cultures that passed through the town are preserved.

The town of Arcos de Jalón is located in the valley of the river Jalón, close to its source. The numerous rivers and streams that cross the municipality mark the natural geography, with lush vegetation that make up the fertile plains, of great scenic richness.

Layna is a small town of stone houses located in the vast Campo Taranz or Layna moor. An extensive plateau at an altitude of 1,000 meters, historically unpopulated and a transition path between Molina de Aragón and Medinaceli. The Layna páramo is mentioned several times in the Cantar de Mio Cid and reference is made to the harshness of transit through this uninhabited area where temperatures of 11 degrees below zero can be reached in winter.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily designed for road cycling, meaning you'll encounter mostly paved surfaces. However, be prepared for some segments that may be unpaved and could present a challenge for a racebike. The scenery is diverse, featuring the spectacular rocky walls and fluvial terraces of the Jalón River gorges, and the historic town of Medinaceli perched on a hill.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Given its distance of approximately 96 km, nearly 1200 meters of elevation gain, and a 'difficult' rating, this route is best suited for experienced road cyclists with very good fitness. Beginners might find the length, climbs, and potential unpaved sections quite challenging.

faqs.question: What cultural and natural attractions can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. The route takes you through the dramatic Gorges of the Jalón River, where you might spot birds of prey. The historic town of Medinaceli is a major highlight, featuring Spain's only Roman triumphal arch with three bays, as well as an Arab Gate and other ancient sites. You'll also pass through the settlements of Arcos de Jalón and Layna.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder conditions of winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go, as conditions can change, especially in mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Medinaceli and Río Jalón circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Medinaceli is a common starting point for routes in this area. You can typically find parking within or near the town, which serves as a good base for exploring the surrounding trails. Consider starting your ride from a location with ample parking to ensure a smooth beginning to your tour.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling tour. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public roads, bringing a dog on a nearly 100 km racebike tour is generally not advisable due to the distance, speed, and potential traffic. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely keep pace and is accustomed to long distances and road environments.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths through natural and historical areas that are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Tierras de Frontera, the Camino del Cid (Senderista), and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, among others. This allows for a varied experience and connects you to a wider network of trails.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through settlements like Medinaceli, Arcos de Jalón, and Layna, you should be able to find places to refuel, such as cafes or small shops. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: For this challenging road cycling tour, you should bring appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet, comfortable cycling attire, and clipless pedals if you use them. Essential items include plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), a first-aid kit, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Given the route's length and difficulty, consider carrying a GPS device or having the komoot app downloaded with the route offline.

faqs.question: Are there any particularly scenic viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: The entire route offers scenic views, particularly through the Gorges of the Jalón River with their dramatic rock formations. The ascent to Medinaceli, though potentially strenuous, rewards you with panoramic views from the historic hilltop town, making it a significant viewpoint.
